Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.'S Application That Exhibit A To Plaintiff Samuel Spangenberg'S Declaration That Was Filed On October 5, 2016 Be Filed Under Seal
Off Calendar Oct-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. BARBARA E. FIGARI OF THE FIGARI LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-626-486-2620 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. ANDREW M. SPURCHISE OF THE LITTLER MENDELSON, P.C., TEL. NO. 1-212-583-2684 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.'S Application That Exhibit A To Plaintiff Samuel Spangenberg'S Declaration That Was Filed On October 5, 2016 Be Filed Under Seal is GRANTED. No opposition filed and Mr. Spangenberg has stipulated to the relief sought by the application. Uber has shown that it has an overriding interest in keeping the contents of exhibit A from the public. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Plaintiffs Paga Claim Pending Arbitration
Off Calendar Oct-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. BARBARA E. FIGARI OF THE FIGARI LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-626-486-2620 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. ANDREW M. SPURCHISE OF THE LITTLER MENDELSON, P.C., TEL. NO. 1-212-583-2684 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Plaintiffs Paga Claim Pending Arbitration is granted. Uber has shown that the parties have an arbitration agreement that covers all of plaintiff Samuel Spangenberg's claims except his PAGA claim. All of the arguments made by Mr. Spangenberg to avoid that agreement lack merit. Perez does not bar arbitration of claims other than a PAGA claim. Mr. Spangenberg's FEHA claims are individual claims, not brought as an agent of DFEH or on behalf of others. Cruz and Broughton are no longer good law. Per the current state of FAA arbitration law, declaratory and injunctive relief requests are arbitrable. While the substantive provisions of the FAA apply, the procedural provisions of CAA apply because the parties' arbitration agreement did not expressly adopt the procedural provisions of the FAA (Valencia v. Smyth (2010) 185 Cal. App. 4th 153) and thus the jury trial provisions of the FAA are not applicable to this case. According to the court's register of action for this case which is publicly available, there is no pleading alleging any claims against the "individual defendants" and thus the purported existence of claims against such non-existent parties is not a basis for plaintiff to avoid arbitration of his claims other than his PAGA claim against Uber. Although the parties' arbitration agreement has some level of procedural unconscionability, Mr. Spangenberg has not shown that any substantive unconscionability exists. The arbitration agreement complies with the requirements of Armendariz, and has sufficient bilaterality. Judicial economy dictates that Mr. Spangenberg's arbitrable claims be resolved prior to the resolution of the PAGA claim. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/hk
